channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Here's how we're coping with high gas prices, according to Costco and Walmart
item.description: Sky-high gas prices have drivers going out of their way for discounts at the pump. Oil executives warn that even higher prices might be on the horizon.
item.content:encoded:
Sky-high gas prices have drivers going out of their way for discounts at the pump. Oil executives warn that even higher prices might be on the horizon.

(Image credit: Paul Sancya)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: CBS' Bari Weiss overhauls '60 Minutes,' fires top producer and reporters
item.description: The new head of CBS News is flexing her muscles at the network's most storied news program: "60 Minutes." We look at the upheaval.
item.content:encoded: The new head of CBS News is flexing her muscles at the network's most storied news program: "60 Minutes." We look at the upheaval.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: FIFA's World Cup ticket sales outraged fans. Now they are under investigation
item.description: FIFA has always had a peculiar way to sell tickets to the World Cup. It never faced any major issues — until prices soared for the 2026 tournament.
item.content:encoded:
FIFA has always had a peculiar way to sell tickets to the World Cup. It never faced any major issues — until prices soared for the 2026 tournament.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: This is how close American households are to the financial edge
item.description: A new report shows that nearly half of U.S. households did not earn enough to cover their necessities in 2024.
item.content:encoded:
A new report shows that nearly half of U.S. households did not earn enough to cover their necessities in 2024.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Trump wants a gas tax holiday. There's a much bigger problem looming
item.description: Suspending the federal gasoline tax could save drivers up to 18.4 cents per gallon. But it would drain the fund meant to cover roadbuilding and repairs — a fund that's already in trouble.
item.content:encoded:
Suspending the federal gasoline tax could save drivers up to 18.4 cents per gallon. But it would drain the fund meant to cover roadbuilding and repairs — a fund that's already in trouble.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: These swing voters are adding high gas prices into their political calculations
item.description: As part of NPR's Swing Shift project checking in with swing voters, these Americans are split on who is to blame for high gas prices but they all agree it has an impact on their personal finances.
item.content:encoded:
As part of NPR's Swing Shift project checking in with swing voters, these Americans are split on who is to blame for high gas prices but they all agree it has an impact on their personal finances.

(Image credit: Illustrations by Tara Anand)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Ferrari unveiled its first EV model. The response has been lukewarm
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks to Mack Hogan of Inside EVs about the lukewarm consumer response to Ferrari's first electric vehicle.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks to Mack Hogan of Inside EVs about the lukewarm consumer response to Ferrari's first electric vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: DOJ charges Google staffer over Polymarket trades netting $1.2 million
item.description: It's the second known case of the federal government filing criminal charges against someone who allegedly used insider information to make a large profit on a prediction market site.
item.content:encoded:
It's the second known case of the federal government filing criminal charges against someone who allegedly used insider information to make a large profit on a prediction market site.

(Image credit: Josh Edelson)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: NPR's newsroom shrinks through buyouts and layoffs
item.description: At least 18 NPR journalists have accepted buyouts and another 10 have been laid off as the public media network attempts to save money and reorganize the newsroom.
item.content:encoded:
At least 18 NPR journalists have accepted buyouts and another 10 have been laid off as the public media network attempts to save money and reorganize the newsroom.

(Image credit: Wanyu Zhang)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: More people are going hungry now than at the height of the pandemic
item.description: Food insecurity affects more families now than during the coronavirus pandemic, according to a new survey from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.
item.content:encoded:
Food insecurity affects more families now than during the coronavirus pandemic, according to a new survey from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

(Image credit: Mark Felix)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley
